events.title: World at ‘perilous moment’ as leaders warn HIV gains are at risk
events.description: Four decades after AIDS first emerged as a global crisis, world leaders, advocates and community representatives gathered at UN Headquarters on Monday issued a stark warning: progress against HIV is slowing just as financial pressures and shrinking support threaten to reverse decades of gains.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 World at ‘perilous moment’ as leaders warn HIV gains are at risk

events.title: At least 13 killed and dozens injured after Qatar gas explosion
events.description: The city's main liquified natural gas (LNG) processing site suffered a "technical accident" in the Ras Laffan industrial zone.
events.ticker_label: 💥 At least 13 killed and dozens injured after Qatar gas explosion

events.title: Iran set to return to mainstream oil market as US temporarily waives sanctions
events.description: As 60 days of peace negotiations between the US and Iran continue, Tehran appears to have won significant concessions from Washington. Chief among those is the waiving of sanctions on Iranian oil exports. US sanctions have been in place for decades, cutting Iran off from mainstream global oil markets and forcing it to sell its oil to China at discount rates. The US says the other side of the deal is that Iran will allow international inspectors access to its nuclear program - Tehran though says it has made no such commitment.

events.title: Iran war day 116: US eases Iran sanctions; Israel kills two in Lebanon
events.description: US eases oil sanctions for 60 days after Iran agrees to allow international nuclear inspections.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran war day 116: US eases Iran sanctions; Israel kills two in Lebanon

events.title: Offshore typhoon threatens Philippine provinces with possible flooding and rough seas
events.description: Philippine authorities have warned of possible flooding, landslides and rough seas in the country’s two northernmost provinces as a powerful typhoon roared offshore
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Offshore typhoon threatens Philippine provinces with possible flooding and rough seas

events.title: CDC to tap $107m in emergency funding for Ebola response in DRC and Uganda
events.description: Number of people infected now tops 1,000 though health officials say the global risk remains low Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) will tap $107m in emergency funding for Ebola outbreak response in the Democratic Republic of the Congo ( DRC ) and Uganda , officials said on Thursday. The continued Ebola outbreak in the DRC comes as Canada, Mexico and the US jointly host the Fifa World Cup , attracting visitors from around the world. The officials said the outbreak, now the third largest on record, required “strong immediate support”, but that the global risk remained low. Continue reading...

events.title: Counterterrorism officials investigating after suspect goes on rampage in alleged hate attack: report
events.description: Counterterrorism officials are investigating after a suspect allegedly went on an anti-Muslim rampage in Edinburgh, leaving five men injured near a mosque and another location.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Counterterrorism officials investigating after suspect goes on rampage in alleged hate attack: report

events.title: Security Council LIVE: Warnings of ‘dangerous cycle of escalation’ in Ukraine
events.description: Deadly drone and missile attacks in Ukraine over the weekend represented a “dangerous cycle of escalation” in Russia’s full-scale four-year-old war, the Security Council heard on Monday from Mohamed Khaled Khiari, Assistant Secretary-General in the Departments of Political and Peacebuilding Affairs and Peace Operations, while Edem Wosornu, crisis response director at the UN relief office, OCHA, warned that “the choices made here can mean the difference between lives saved or lives lost.”
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Security Council LIVE: Warnings of ‘dangerous cycle of escalation’ in Ukraine

events.title: Myanmar: Aid decline compounds suffering amid ongoing military attacks
events.description: A drop in humanitarian assistance is worsening the suffering of millions of people in Myanmar after five years of conflict-related violence, the UN human rights office, OHCHR, said in a report published on Monday.

events.title: 6 dead, 39 shot in Chicago bloodbath as Trump touts DC-style cleanup where National Guard stepped in

events.title: Sweat, tears and camaraderie as 20,000 runners take on world’s largest ultramarathon
events.description: For one day every June, South Africa’s searing racial inequality seems to melt away at Comrades race In the early morning dark, thousands of runners waited, jostling with anticipation. South Africa’s national anthem rang out. Then the haunting swell of Shosholoza , first sung by Zimbabwean migrant workers in South Africa’s goldmines. Finally, that unmistakable, spine-tingling piano: Chariots of Fire. Runners gather before the start of the marathon Continue reading...

events.title: Tesla crash that killed a woman under US federal investigation

events.title: Global Ebola cases top 1,000 as UN races to reach DR Congo’s most vulnerable
events.description: As global confirmed Ebola cases reach 1,000, nearly three million children and adolescents are at risk in the eastern part of the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), while efforts increase to treat prisoners near the epicentre of the current outbreak, UN agencies warned on Monday.

events.title: ‘Better future for Syria remains within reach,’ UN Security Council hears

events.title: Australia ‘sleepwalking’ into AI crisis and ‘tech bro free-for-all’, says Greens senator
events.description: Sarah Hanson Young’s warning comes as David Pocock urges government to prevent firms using Australian content to train AI models Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The independent senator David Pocock has challenged the Albanese government to prevent tech giants using Australian content to train AI models as cabinet considers proposals to change copyright rules for the rapidly developing technology. His call came as the Greens senator Sarah Hanson-Young called for a moratorium on the building and approval of new datacentres in Australia until “we get the regulations right”. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Australia ‘sleepwalking’ into AI crisis and ‘tech bro free-for-all’, says Greens senator

events.title: US-Iran deal: Pakistan's diplomatic masterstroke?
events.description: By stepping in as mediator in the Iran war, Pakistan has managed to elevate its status from a regional player to a global diplomatic stakeholder — with a little help from China and its friends in the Gulf.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US-Iran deal: Pakistan's diplomatic masterstroke?

events.title: France issues red alert as heatwave breaks records and death toll mounts
events.description: France experienced its hottest-ever night Monday into Tuesday as Prime Minister Sébastien Lecornu said 40 people had drowned across the country since June 18 as they sought to escape the record heat. In a country without widespread air-conditioning, the scorching temperatures have led to school closures, cancelled events and disrupted rail services.
events.ticker_label: 🚑 France issues red alert as heatwave breaks records and death toll mounts

events.title: Resistance fighter Marc Bloch becomes first historian to enter France's Panthéon
events.description: This Tuesday June 23, Marc Bloch will be inducted into Paris's Panthéon mausoleum. A leading 20th-century historian, he co-founded the French academic journal "Les Annales" and was also a soldier in both World Wars. Bloch, who was Jewish, joined the French Resistance during World War II and was tortured and executed by the Gestapo in 1944. Throughout his life, he embodied both intellectual rigour and an unwavering commitment to the French Republic. FRANCE 24's Florence Gaillard reports, with Florent Marchais.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Resistance fighter Marc Bloch becomes first historian to enter France's Panthéon

events.title: Climate crisis: UN chief lays out solutions blueprint for clean energy transition
events.description: As a deadly heatwave continued to grip Europe on Tuesday, UN Secretary-General António Guterres issued an impassioned appeal for more ambitious global action on climate change caused by fossil fuels, to prevent irreversible damage.

events.title: UN welcomes fresh Lebanon ceasefire reports as rights experts urge Iran accountability

events.title: Ghana conference calls for formal apology for transatlantic slave trade
events.description: Global framework for reparatory justice adopted at event includes demand for compensation and debt relief More than money: the logic of slavery reparations A global framework for reparatory justice has been adopted at a conference in Ghana, as African and Caribbean leaders demanded formal apologies from countries that benefited from the transatlantic slave trade. Heads of state and government and other officials formally approved the strategy on Friday at a gathering in a hotel in the capital, Accra, which was the first major meeting since the adoption of the landmark United Nations (UN) resolution declaring the trafficking of enslaved Africans as the gravest crime against humanity. Continue reading...

events.title: Fact check: AI fakes around the World Cup spread political narratives

events.ticker_label: 🪖 Fact check: AI fakes around the World Cup spread political narratives

events.title: From malaria to energy: Why solutions from the Global South aren’t reaching the people who need them most
events.description: Innovators – especially in the Global South – are too often locked out of funding and opportunity despite offering solutions to some of the world’s most pressing challenges.

events.title: France faces historically hot temperatures as heatwave carries on
events.description: Millions of people across France woke up drenched in sweat on June 23 after another night of scorching heat, with most of the population exposed to extreme and exceptional temperatures. In a country without widespread air-conditioning, schools, trains and sporting events remain impacted, while some 20 drowning deaths have been reported since the weekend. FRANCE 24's Caroline Baum tells us more from the Eiffel Tower in Paris.

events.title: They bought an abandoned 'ghost house' in the Japanese countryside

events.title: Weather tracker: Severe thunderstorms sweep Europe and east Asia
events.description: Strong winds and heavy rain batter Slovenia, while France experiences atypical heatwave Severe thunderstorms swept across the Balkans last week, bringing widespread destruction to parts of the region. The storms developed as unstable hot air lingered over the Adriatic Sea while a cold front plunged south-eastward. The front began its journey on 10 June in Slovenia, where the Slovenian Environment Agency recorded 65mph gusts at Ljubljana airport. Heavy rain also fell widely across the region with 23mm reported in Kranj. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: ✈️ Weather tracker: Severe thunderstorms sweep Europe and east Asia

events.title: Far-right millionaire wins Colombia’s razor-tight presidential election
events.description: Leftwing opponent alleges vote count irregularities after Trump-endorsed lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella secures narrow majority A Trump-admiring far-right millionaire lawyer and self-styled “outsider” has defeated a leftwing senator by a razor-thin margin to win Colombia’s presidential runoff, in an election that promises to mark a dramatic shift in the country’s decades-long armed conflict. With 99.99% of ballots counted in the preliminary vote tally, the far-right lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella had secured 12.96m votes, or 49.66%, just 250,830 more than the leftist senator Iván Cepeda, who received 12.7m votes, or 48.7%. A further 1.6% of ballots were cast blank. Continue reading...

events.title: Alan Greenspan, the legendary former Federal Reserve chair, dies
events.description: During his chairmanship, Greenspan was celebrated as possibly the best central banker in history. But later, his reputation was tarnished by the worst financial crisis since the Great Depression.

events.title: Israel, Lebanon to hold new round of US-hosted talks
events.description: Israel and Lebanon prepare for a fresh round of negotiations in Washington. Iran says negotiating groups to oversee sanctions, nuclear issues, economic reconstruction and implementation. DW has the latest.

events.title: French celebrate win against Iraq despite two-hour delay caused by storm
events.description: The weather in the US tried to put a stop to the France-Iraq game at the World Cup on June 22... and it did: the game had to be halted for two hours as a huge thunderstorm hit in Philadelphia, where it was being held. France ended up winning 3-0, with two of them coming from French superstar Kylian Mbappé. But nothing could deter French fans from celebrating.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ French celebrate win against Iraq despite two-hour delay caused by storm

events.title: World Cup 2026: France into last 32 with win over Iraq despite two-hour weather delay
events.description: With a second win in two matches, Les Bleus secured their place in the World Cup round of 32. Although they were never really troubled by Iraq (3-0), France were mainly disrupted by a storm over Philadelphia, which suspended the match for almost two hours between the first and second halves.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ World Cup 2026: France into last 32 with win over Iraq despite two-hour weather delay

events.title: Vietnam police rescue hundreds of cats stolen for meat by crime ring

More than 40 cats were reunited with their owners after the multiday operation last week, but several dozen of those rescued have died due to the harsh conditions in which they were found, the groups said. Continue reading...

events.title: Andrew Hastie to get extra security, citing One Nation campaign against him
events.description: Hastie tells colleagues home affairs minister did not disclose the threats made against him, but he has been targeted online for his views on Ben Roberts-Smith Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Liberal frontbencher Andrew Hastie is set to receive extra security in what he believes is a response to a One Nation-fuelled campaign against him, including over his stance on the Ben Roberts-Smith war crimes allegations. At a partyroom meeting on Tuesday, Hastie told colleagues that the home affairs minister, Tony Burke, had advised him that he had been identified for security measures at his home and electorate office. Continue reading...

US Vice president JD Vance said it laid a good foundation for a deal to end the war. The parties agreed to establish mechanisms to open the strait of Hormuz but it is unclear who will control it.

events.title: US waives Iran oil sanctions as part of 60-day peace negotiations
events.description: The United States temporarily waived sanctions on Iranian oil exports on Monday, opening the door for Tehran to return to global energy markets as negotiations between the two countries continue. In this edition, we look at the impact this has had on oil prices, while also examining major job cuts at Oracle linked to AI-driven restructuring and a sharp sell-off in technology stocks that's rippled from Wall Street to Asian and European markets.

events.title: Armed group in Nigeria kills at least 20 people and exchanges gunfire with police

events.description: Air pollution spiked to unhealthy levels around the world in 2021, according to a new report.

events.title: 'Temu version': World's largest Messi statue mocked online
events.description: PRESS REVIEW – Tuesday, June 23: This Tuesday marks 10 years since millions of British people voted for Brexit. The milestone comes one day after Prime Minister Keir Starmer's tearful resignation. Also, the internet goes crazy for "hot podium guy", a handsome sound engineer who sets up the lectern in front of 10 Downing Street for each big announcement. Indian leader Narendra Modi inaugurates three new Indian-made naval vessels in a move seeking to counter China's growing influence. Finally, a new statue in honour of Lionel Messi is ridiculed as a "Temu" of Messi statues!

events.title: Whistleblower investigating Ecuadorian president’s family business was murdered, activists say
events.description: Monika Silva Koniuszek died from a blow to the head and strangulation, a postmortem found, despite government claim of suicide Campaigners in Ecuador say a Polish anti-corruption activist who investigated allegations against the family business of the country’s rightwing president was murdered to silence her. Monika Silva Koniuszek, 41, was found dead in her home in Montañita, a coastal town in Ecuador’s Santa Elena province. The single mother of daughters aged four and nine, was found on the floor with a noose around her neck on 8 June. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Whistleblower investigating Ecuadorian president’s family business was murdered, activists say

events.title: World Cup 2026: Tartan Army turns Little Havana into ‘Little Scotland’
events.description: Thousands of Scotland supporters turned Miami’s Little Havana neighbourhood into “Little Scotland” on Monday, with the Tartan Army marching through the streets with bagpipes and drums ahead of their match with Brazil in the city later this week.

events.title: 'Flamingo Revolution': Albanian protests swell against Trump-linked resort
events.description: Albanians have been gathering in the streets of Tijana ever day for over three weeks. The so-called 'flamingo revolution' started in reaction to the construction of luxury resorts linked to the Trump family in the south of the country on an island where the protected birds nest. France 24 journalist David Gilberg followed a Franco-Albanian woman named Iris as she protested in her home country this weekend. FRANCE 24 went on the ground and spoke to some of them.

events.title: Israel’s targeting of Palestinian children establishes ‘genocidal intent’ in Gaza, UN probe says
events.description: Israeli authorities and security forces "deliberately targeted and killed" children in what has become a key factor in its "genocidal intent" to "destroy the larger Palestinian group in Gaza", a UN inquiry revealed Tuesday. Israel has slammed the report as "defamatory" and a "libellous sham".

events.title: Guinness crowns Canberra town crier as the world's loudest person at 122.4 decibels

events.description: Some people travel the world in search of adventure, while others seek out natural wonders, cultural landmarks or culinary experiences. But French photographer François Prost was looking for something altogether different during his recent road trip across America: strip clubs.

events.title: Kenyan minister orders halt to construction of US Ebola facility
events.description: Decision comes after Aden Duale was held in contempt for ignoring previous high court ruling to stop work Kenya’s health minister has told a court he has ordered a halt to preparations for a US-run Ebola quarantine facility, after being held in contempt for ignoring a previous stop-work order. Many Kenyans strongly oppose the plan and deadly protests have erupted since the facility was announced in May for US citizens evacuated from the Democratic Republic of Congo, which is grappling with a large-scale Ebola outbreak. Continue reading...

events.title: Lost memoir of Hiroshima survivor found after decades in US archive
events.description: Written in 1947, Kiyoshi Tanimoto’s account of the horrors of the atomic bomb attack will be published in August and is being made into a film The memoir of a man who survived the horrors of Hiroshima is to be published for the first time this summer after its discovery in a US archive. The 230-page memoir was written almost 80 years ago by Kiyoshi Tanimoto, who witnessed the city’s destruction after the atomic bomb was dropped in 1945. He will now be portrayed in a feature film by Takehiro Hira, whose acclaimed roles include the detective in the Netflix Japanese-British drama Giri/Haji. Pre-production begins in November, ahead of the shoot in February 2027. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Lost memoir of Hiroshima survivor found after decades in US archive

events.title: Cult leader William Kamm and wife groomed young girl for a decade, Sydney court told
events.description: The 76-year-old allegedly ordered he should have 12 wives to ‘repopulate’ the world. He and his wife deny the charges Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The leader of a cult and his wife allegedly groomed a young girl over a decade so she could bear 45 of his children in a post-apocalyptic world. William Kamm, also known as “Little Pebble”, was the leader of the Order of Saint Charbel, a religious cult run from a property on the New South Wales south coast. Continue reading...

events.title: US: Trump signs new executive orders to boost quantum computing

events.title: World Cup: Amiri another wildcard for Nagelsmann's Germany

events.title: Messi becomes World Cup all-time goal scorer in game against Austria
events.description: France's second World Cup game belonged to Mbappé as he scored twice, moving into a tie for second in World Cup goals. Argentina won its second game against Austria, with two goals scored by Messi. Norway's Haaland also scored two goals in the game against Senegal, whose fate now depends on its game against Iraq. FRANCE 24's James Vasina tells us what to know.

events.title: Billionaire to invest in ocean protection as UK and US scale back funding
events.description: Michael Bloomberg has pledged $260m (£196m) to conserve the world's oceans, as major funders including the US and UK scale back science and conservation budgets.

events.title: Turkey detains 209 in raids in the capital ahead of July's NATO summit
events.description: Security forces in Turkey have detained over 200 people with suspected links to extremist groups, including the Islamic State

events.title: Priced out of World Cup games, Mexican fans take celebrations into their own hands
events.description: Mexicans are reclaiming the FIFA World Cup with street celebrations as high ticket prices keep many out of stadiums

events.title: Merlin the duck steals the spotlight at President Sheinbaum's news briefing
events.description: Merlín the duck, Mexico’s unofficial World Cup mascot, stole the spotlight at President Claudia Sheinbaum’s news briefing

events.title: UNESCO launches consultation on fair payment for news in the digital age
events.description: UN cultural agency UNESCO has launched a global consultation process to inform its Draft Guidance on Fair Compensation for News, particularly as online platforms and artificial intelligence (AI) increasingly rely on journalistic content.

events.title: Old and new challenges for the Human Rights Council as it turns 20
events.description: It’s 20 years to the day since the UN Human Rights Council began its work as the world’s principal forum tasked with promoting and defending fundamental rights everywhere, particularly the world’s most vulnerable people.

events.title: EXCLUSIVE: Meet the man Israel chose to be its first-ever ambassador to the Christian world
events.description: Ambassador George Deek explains the mission behind his new role as special envoy to the Christian world and Israel's efforts to strengthen the Judeo-Christian bond.

events.title: Drowning deaths soar in France as Europe buckles in peak of heatwave
events.description: Forty people have drowned in heatwave-related deaths in France since last Thursday, Prime Minister Sébastien Lecornu says.

events.title: Palestinian children targeted in genocide, war crimes in Gaza: UN inquiry
events.description: UN commission of inquiry says Israel's deliberate targeting of Palestinian children in Gaza resulted in genocide.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Palestinian children targeted in genocide, war crimes in Gaza: UN inquiry

events.title: Retail spending fell in March as consumers pull back
events.description: Spending at US retailers fell in March as consumers pulled back amid recessionary fears fueled by the banking crisis.

events.title: Montreal mayor calls for end to random police checks amid racial profiling investigation
events.description: City’s police force faces investigation of 16 officers accused of disproportionately targeting Black and Arab residents Montreal’s mayor has called for a halt to random police checks as the city’s police force grapples with an internal investigation into racism and racial profiling by 16 officers. Mayor Soraya Martinez Ferrada told reporters last week that her husband, who is Black, had been repeatedly stopped by police while driving. Continue reading...

events.title: Dutch PM apologises for Moluccan soldiers’ mistreatment after Indonesian independence
events.description: Rob Jetten acknowledges grief and pain of Moluccan families as crowdfunded monument unveiled in Rotterdam The Dutch prime minister, Rob Jetten, has formally apologised for the “heartless” mistreatment of thousands of Moluccan soldiers who fought for the Dutch colonial army during Indonesia’s struggle for independence. About 12,500 people – men who had served in the Royal Dutch East Indies and their families – came from a group of Indonesian islands to the Netherlands in 1951, many having been given no choice. They thought it would be a temporary evacuation after Indonesia had won independence. Continue reading...

events.title: Iran's president heads to Pakistan as U.S.-Iran teams work on war-ending deal
events.description: President Masoud Pezeshkian's visit to Islamabad comes as technical teams were working on details of the deal, following high-level negotiations in Switzerland on Monday led by US Vice President JD Vance and Iran's parliamentary speaker, Mohammad Bagher Qalibaf.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran's president heads to Pakistan as U.S.-Iran teams work on war-ending deal

events.title: Iran-US talks to end war beset by tension, mistrust
events.description: While both sides claim "encouraging progress," experts say there's a long way to go to turn the shaky diplomatic framework into a durable agreement.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran-US talks to end war beset by tension, mistrust

events.title: Europe faces warmest year on record as heatwave batters continent
events.description: Europeans face sweltering temperatures once again, as another heatwave brings disruption in schools, rail services and sport events, as officials fear heat-related health risks. Temperatures are expected to hit 39 degrees celsius, heading to be the hottest year on record. According to the WHO, over 200 000 people have died from heat-related causes in Europe in the past four years.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Europe faces warmest year on record as heatwave batters continent

events.title: Philippines temporarily blocks gaming app used by suspect in deadly school shooting
events.description: Philippine authorities are temporarily blocking an online gaming app after a school shooting

events.title: AI is getting women wrong as gender bias persists, data reveals
events.description: As artificial intelligence reshapes how people work, communicate and access information, UN Women warned on Monday that the technology is reproducing old gender stereotypes, which amplify online abuse and leave women out of the decisions that will define the digital future.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 AI is getting women wrong as gender bias persists, data reveals

events.title: Afghanistan in crisis: Drought, malnutrition and a worsening humanitarian situation
events.description: Imagine being one of a family of nine and sitting down to a meal of potato peelings and other scraps, boiled up into a soup. This is the harsh reality for many of Afghanistan’s most vulnerable families, forced on them by climate change and drought, widespread malnutrition and increasing restrictions on women, since the Taliban overran Kabul in 2021.

events.title: South Carolina fitness trainer’s autopsy raises more questions about mysterious death
events.description: Autopsy of South Carolina fitness trainer Elena Katherine Moore found no signs of trauma. Cause of death remains pending as toxicology results are awaited.

events.title: Vigil planned for 18-year-old tourist killed in Central Park horse-drawn carriage incident

events.title: Keir Starmer reportedly considering stepping down as PM and could announce timetable for departure

events.title: Israeli ambassador, UN official shout over each other in tense exchange over sexual violence blacklist
events.description: Israeli Ambassador Danny Danon erupted at a U.N. official during a tense heated exchange over Israel's placement on a U.N. sexual violence blacklist.

events.title: Red heat alerts issued in France, Italy and Spain as 40C temperatures forecast
events.description: The heatwave conditions are forecast to intensify in the coming days across central and western Europe.

events.title: Karl Stefanovic praises UK far-right activist Tommy Robinson’s ‘tenacity’ and ‘courage’ in latest podcast
events.description: Robinson says Pauline Hanson has ‘carried on fighting’ despite backlash in episode of the Karl Stefanovic Show Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The Channel Nine TV presenter Karl Stefanovic has praised UK far-right activist Tommy Robinson’s “tenacity” and “courage” in his latest eponymous podcast episode, in which Robinson rails against “the media and the establishment”, Islam, multiculturalism and hate speech laws. Stefanovic posted a clip on social media on Tuesday morning Australian time in which he is walking down a London street with his arm slung around Robinson, who has spearheaded recent nationalist demonstrations in the UK. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Karl Stefanovic praises UK far-right activist Tommy Robinson’s ‘tenacity’ and ‘courage’ in latest podcast

events.title: Silicon Valley Bank collapse renews calls to address disparities impacting entrepreneurs of color
events.description: When customers at Silicon Valley Bank rushed to withdraw billions of dollars last month, venture capitalist Arlan Hamilton stepped in to help some of the founders of color who panicked about losing access to payroll funds.

events.title: U.S. lifts Iran oil sanctions. And, federal judge rules SAVE voter tool unlawful
events.description: The U.S. has temporarily lifted oil sanctions on Iran as peace talks continue. And, a federal judge ruled that the Trump administration's data system, known as SAVE, is unlawful.

events.title: Turkey arrests more than 200 in crackdown before NATO summit
events.description: The Ankara prosecutor's office said 209 people were detained, while 32 others remained at large. Turkey has announced a ban on demonstrations amid preparations for next month's NATO summit in the capital.

events.title: Alan Greenspan, longtime head of the US Federal Reserve, dies aged 100
events.description: Alan Greenspan, the longtime head of the Federal Reserve and influential economist, has died aged 100.

events.title: ‘Institutional threat’: election of far-right leader raises fears for democracy in Colombia
events.description: Trump-admiring Abelardo de la Espriella has vowed to ‘disembowel’ the left and kill criminals like ‘rats and cockroaches’ When more than 20 women accused a Colombian evangelical pastor in 2012 of sexually abusing them, the defendant’s lawyer sought to discredit the allegations by telling the court that they were “ trepadoras ” – a pejorative term meaning social climbers. He ultimately secured his client’s acquittal – although the case remains under review by the supreme court – but footage of the remark resurfaced during Colombia’s presidential campaign, sparking outrage among many progressive voters. Continue reading...

events.title: A red alert over France, and heat that may rewrite the record books
events.description: France is experiencing scorching heat, with most of the population exposed to extreme temperatures. The heat is expected to last until at least the end of the week.

events.title: Gunman kills an officer at a Montreal hotel before being shot dead, police say
events.description: Police say a suspect armed with a long gun opened fire at a Montreal hotel, killing a police officer before officers returned fire, killing him.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Gunman kills an officer at a Montreal hotel before being shot dead, police say

events.title: Europe on red alert for record-breaking heat
events.description: Dozens of people have drowned in France and Germany in recent days as people seek relief from "oppressive" heat. In Italy, more than a dozen cities are on high alert for extreme heat. DW has the latest.

events.title: US: Trump threatens prison for Reflecting Pool 'vandalism'
events.description: The Trump administration spent $14.7 million repainting the pool "American flag blue" ahead of US' 250th anniversary celebrations. Weeks later, the paint is peeling off and algae has turned the water green.

events.title: Why is Bulgaria threatening to veto EU's Russia sanctions?
events.description: Bulgarian PM Rumen Radev has vowed to veto the EU's latest sanctions against Russia because they include Russian Orthodox Patriarch Kirill and former Lukoil president Vagit Alekperov. What's behind his opposition?

events.title: Protests erupt in Colombia as far-right set to win presidential election
events.description: Protests have erupted in Colombia against the election of the far-right candidate Abelardo de la Espriella. Though election officials have yet to formally announce a winner, with almost all of the votes counted, the pro-Trump lawyer and businessman is set to defeat his left-wing opponent Ivan Cepeda. Trailing behind by around 250 000 votes, the left-wing senator told supporters his team would be challenging results from tens of thousands of voting stations.

events.title: Afghan Taliban set for EU migration talks slammed by rights groups
events.description: The EU is expected to hold talks on returning failed Afghan asylum-seekers with a Taliban delegation in Brussels Tuesday after Belgium issued one-day visas for the team, overriding criticisms from human rights groups. The 27-member bloc does not recognise the Taliban administration, but the European Commission invited a team from Kabul for discussions amid a push to boost deportations.

events.title: Gunman kills a Montreal officer at a Hilton hotel before being shot dead, police say
events.description: Police say a suspect armed with a long gun opened fire at a Montreal hotel, killing a police officer before officers returned fire, killing him
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Gunman kills a Montreal officer at a Hilton hotel before being shot dead, police say

events.title: Fire tears through building in northern India, killing 14, mostly students
events.description: A fire has torn through a commercial building in Lucknow, India, killing at least 14 people, mostly students

events.title: Ebola in DR Congo: One month on, scaled up response remains insufficient
events.description: One month after the Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak was declared in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) and Uganda, case numbers continue to rise.

events.title: Coast Guard helicopter crashes during Alaska training mission, injuring four crew members
events.description: A Coast Guard MH-60 Jayhawk helicopter crashed during routine training near Sitka, Alaska, with four crew members sustaining minor injuries.

events.title: Supreme Court reinstates conviction in 1979 Etan Patz murder
events.description: America's high court has reinstated the conviction of a man in connection with the 1979 disappearance of six-year-old child Etan Patz in New York City.

events.title: Not only is Lake Powell's water level plummeting because of drought, its total capacity is shrinking, too
events.description: Lake Powell, the second-largest human-made reservoir in the US, has lost nearly 7% of its potential storage capacity since 1963, when Glen Canyon Dam was built, a new report shows.

events.title: Canadian healthcare staff decry ‘cruel hoax’ after scam email promises paid day off
events.description: Unions condemn ‘insensitive’ internal cybersecurity test sent to healthcare workers in Newfoundland and Labrador For years, healthcare staff in the Canadian province of Newfoundland and Labrador have felt overworked and underappreciated. Turnover, burnout and thinning resources were pushing workers in the sector to a breaking point. So when the email titled “June Holiday” arrived in thousands of inboxes, they felt a moment of overdue joy. Continue reading...

events.title: Vondrousova, ex-Wimbledon champion, insists she has ‘never doped’
events.description: Vondrousova, who won Wimbledon in 2023, was issued a four-year ban from tennis after she refused to submit a ⁠sample.

events.title: Look of the Week: Blackpink headline Coachella in Korean hanboks
events.description: Bringing the second day of this year's Coachella to a close, K-Pop girl group Blackpink made history Saturday night when they became the first Asian act to ever headline the festival. To a crowd of, reportedly, over 125,000 people, Jennie, Jisoo, Lisa and Rosé used the ground-breaking moment to pay homage to Korean heritage by arriving onstage in hanboks: a traditional type of dress.

events.title: How Playboy cut ties with Hugh Hefner to create a post-MeToo brand
events.description: Hugh Hefner launched Playboy Magazine 70 years ago this year. The first issue included a nude photograph of Marilyn Monroe, which he had purchased and published without her knowledge or consent.

events.title: Ex-Olympian denies vandalising Trump's $14m reflective pool project
events.description: Donald Trump has claimed vandals are responsible for issues with the Reflecting Pool in Washington DC following a refurbishment costing more than $14m (£10.5m).

events.title: Powering resilience: How Indonesia is building a more secure energy future
events.description: As countries grapple with energy price shocks, supply disruptions and the growing impacts of climate change, energy security has become a pressing concern.

events.title: Art and culture can both fuel and counter hate, UN discussion hears on Juneteenth
events.description: From a gold collar worn by an enslaved African to music preserved for nearly a century after the Holocaust, a staged reconstruction of a hate radio broadcast or porcelain coffee cups laid out in participatory remembrance, artists in a United Nations discussion on Friday used powerful images to underscore how culture can shape the fight against hatred.

events.title: Chaos erupts on American Airlines flight as unruly passenger allegedly bites fellow traveler midair
events.description: A pilot on American Airlines Flight 3046 reported a passenger bit someone and tried to fight others, requesting EMTs and law enforcement upon landing.

events.title: Trump says UK's Starmer will resign, wishes him well amid reports of PM's political future

events.title: Big-box stores could help slash emissions and save millions by putting solar panels on roofs. Why aren't more of them doing it?
events.description: As the US attempts to wean itself off its heavy reliance on fossil fuels and shift to cleaner energy sources, many experts are eyeing a promising solution: your neighborhood big-box stores and shopping malls.

events.title: Serious failings found in handling of earlier complaint against main suspect in girl's killing
events.description: Three weeks after the body of 11-year-old Lyhanna was found following her disappearance from the French town of Fleurance, investigators have uncovered serious failings in the handling of an earlier complaint against the main suspect. The Interior Minister has sought disciplinary action against two gendarmerie officers, while the Prime Minister said the inspection revealed that "the chain of protection failed."

events.title: UK facing 'pivotal moment' as it prepares for 7th PM since Brexit vote
events.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer won his job after leading his party to a massive victory in 2024. Less than two years later, he resigned as his party rebelled in the wake of widespread losses in local elections. It coincides with the tenth anniversary of the Brexit vote, which led to the resignation of Conservative Prime Minister David Cameron. FRANCE 24's Benedicte Paviot and Rochelle Ferguson-Bouyahi look at how the UK's relationship with the EU will weigh on Starmer's successor, and what led Starmer to resignation.

events.title: Keir Starmer resigns as British prime minister after devastating Labour revolt and local election losses
events.description: British PM Keir Starmer announced his resignation after Labour's devastating local election losses and a mounting revolt from within his own party ranks.

events.title: Zambia ex-president's family wins latest legal battle over what should happen to his body

events.title: Scientists identify secret ingredient in Leonardo da Vinci paintings
events.description: "Old Masters" such as Leonardo da Vinci, Sandro Botticelli and Rembrandt may have used proteins, especially egg yolk, in their oil paintings, according to a new study.

events.title: 'A definitive backslide.' Inside fashion's worrying runway trend
events.description: Now that the Fall-Winter 2023 catwalks have been disassembled, it's clear one trend was more pervasive than any collective penchant for ruffles, pleated skirts or tailored coats.

events.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million
events.description: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

events.title: Here's the real reason to turn on airplane mode when you fly
events.description: We all know the routine by heart: "Please ensure your seats are in the upright position, tray tables stowed, window shades are up, laptops are stored in the overhead bins and electronic devices are set to flight mode."

events.title: Barbados prime minister announces manifesto for slavery reparations
events.description: Updated document, which emphasises harm done to African women, is being considered by other Caribbean countries Barbados’s prime minister, Mia Mottley, has announced a new manifesto from Caribbean leaders asserting the “moral, ethical and legal case” for reparations over damage caused by hundreds of years of enslavement. Mottley was speaking at a “historic” conference in Ghana to advance the push for reparatory justice after the United Nations adopted a landmark resolution declaring the trafficking of enslaved Africans as the gravest crime against humanity. Continue reading...

events.title: Lululemon apologises after Japanese drum row at Great Wall yoga event
events.description: Online uproar follows Canadian brand’s use of taiko drum at sponsored festival held to celebrate Chinese culture The activewear brand Lululemon has apologised after a promotional event held on the Great Wall of China appeared to mistakenly feature a Japanese drum, prompting an uproar. The Canadian-headquartered company, known for its upmarket leggings, has been growing rapidly in China and arranged for a yoga festival to take place in late May on a section of the wall near Beijing. Continue reading...

events.title: Angus Taylor won’t say if he supports multiculturalism
events.description: Treasurer says Coalition playing ‘footsie with the monoculture language’ after Liberal leader sidesteps questions at press conference Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Angus Taylor has repeatedly refused to say whether he supports multiculturalism in Australia, sidestepping five questions at a press conference about his views and claiming “there’s all these vague words running around”. The treasurer, Jim Chalmers, accused the opposition leader of “playing footsie” with One Nation and Pauline Hanson’s call for a “monoculture” in Australia , as Labor ministers committed to backing multiculturalism. Continue reading...

events.title: Britain left the EU 10 years ago. Its politics has been an unruly mess
events.description: Brexit fractured the European Union, and broke British politics. A decade on, millions of voters have deserted the two big parties for alternatives.

events.title: UK PM Keir Starmer resigns. And, US and Iran agree to roadmap for final deal
events.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced his resignation on Monday. And, the U.S. and Iran have agreed to a "roadmap" to reach a final deal within 60 days.

events.title: Keir Starmer has resigned, paving way for a 7th U.K. prime minister in 10 years
events.description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer is stepping down. His likely successor, Andy Burnham, a popular former mayor, would become the U.K.'s seventh prime minister in 10 years.

events.title: Europe's defense ambitions get reality check
events.description: European countries have been trying to scale up and integrate their military capabilities, but their current defense production and procurement model is not fit for purpose, say experts.

events.title: EU hosts Taliban for migrant return talks in Brussels
events.description: The EU is hosting Taliban officials after Belgium granted temporary visas for talks on returning failed Afghan asylum-seekers. The meeting is one of the bloc's highest-level contacts with the Taliban since 2021.

events.title: Germany news: Merz hails pension reform proposals
events.description: Chancellor Merz says a proposed pension reform package should be fully implemented. A survey has shown that almost half of teachers in Germany find pupils' behavior stressful. DW has more.

events.title: More than a unibrow: The enduring appeal of Frida Kahlo
events.description: She shunned feminine beauty ideals, was bisexual and a political radical, all while being brutally honest in her art. A major Frida Kahlo exhibition opens at London's Tate Modern.

events.title: Germany's pension plans draw praise and outrage
events.description: A 33-point plan to reform Germany's pension system has drawn praise and criticism in almost equal measure. Centrist politicians have called it a good compromise, but opposition parties on the left and right are unhappy.

events.title: India news: US waiver clears way for Iran oil purchase
events.description: Iran has contacted Indian refiners to sell its oil after the US temporarily lifted sanctions on Tehran. Meanwhile, an Indian startup founder is going to head Meta's WhatsApp. Follow DW for more.

events.title: Meet Andy Burnham, Britain's likely next prime minister
events.description: Now that British Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced his resignation, all eyes are on Andy Burnham. The former mayor of Manchester is seen as the favorite to succeed Starmer to lead the United Kingdom.

events.title: Germany and UK rebuilding ties 10 years after Brexit vote
events.description: Ten years after the referendum, the two countries are strengthening their ties once again. The stability of these ties is now being put to the test with Keir Starmer to be replaced as prime minister.

events.title: How inflation undermines Nigeria's sugar tax
events.description: Nigeria's sugary drinks tax was meant to stop rising diabetes rates, but soaring inflation has eroded its impact. Experts say the levy is too small to change behavior and urge higher rates and reforms.

events.title: 75 years of Germany's Goethe-Institut
events.description: For 75 years, the Goethe-Institut has stood for cultural exchange, education and partnerships around the globe — even under challenging political conditions.

events.title: How Andy Burnham can become the next UK prime minister
events.description: Now that Keir Starmer has said he's stepping down as Prime Minister, there's only one name that's circulating to take his place. But Andy Burnham still has to be officially anointed in a vote by Labour party members. Starmer has said that he wants the new leader in place by 1st of September, when parliament returns from recess.

events.title: England: Lawn Mower Championships: 'Proper British fun'
events.description: Dozens of speed demons are competing at the British Lawn Mower Championships in Chichester, southern England.These mowers have been modified and have had their blades removed for optimal speed. They can reach an impressive 55mph (88kmph) at top speed. While often seen as a fun motorsport, the racing is incredibly physical.
events.ticker_label: 💥 England: Lawn Mower Championships: 'Proper British fun'

events.title: Former Wimbledon champion given four-year suspension
events.description: Former Wimbledon champion Marketa Vondrousova has been handed a four-year suspension after she refused an anti-doping test.

events.title: Man arrested over collecting human body parts taken from cemeteries, police say
events.description: Police in Hungary have arrested a 30-year-old man for collecting human body parts

events.title: Estranged husband of former Scottish leader sentenced to more than 5 years in prison
events.description: The estranged husband of former Scottish First Minister Nicola Sturgeon has been sentenced to five years and three months in prison

events.title: Afghan Taliban to hold rare, closed-door talks with EU officials on deportations
events.description: A Taliban delegation is in Brussels for talks with the European Union, focusing on deportations, according to a Taliban official

events.title: How Brexit broke British politics
events.description: Brexit fractured the European Union, and it broke British politics

events.title: How Starmer went from Labour Party hero to calling it quits within 2 years
events.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer has admitted defeat, resigning less than two years after leading his party to a massive victory in 2024

events.title: Northern Ireland's former unionist leader convicted of decades-old child sex abuse claims
events.description: Jeffrey Donaldson, former leader of Northern Ireland’s largest unionist party, has been convicted of rape and sex abuse charges involving two girls

events.title: How Keir Starmer will be replaced as UK prime minister and who could succeed him
events.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer has conceded that he has lost the support of his party members in Parliament and will step down as soon as his Labour Party chooses a successor, possibly as soon as next month

events.title: For one young refugee in Uganda, basketball is more than a game
events.description: When 19-year-old Stephane Kulimushi looks around the basketball court in Kampala where he trains young refugees, he sees more than players.

events.title: Charlie Kirk assassination suspect's lawyers mirror notorious killer's tactical delay strategy: fmr prosecutor
events.description: Tyler Robinson's defense in the Charlie Kirk assassination case allegedly mirrors Bryan Kohberger's legal playbook, a former prosecutor says.

events.title: FBI brings back fugitive accused in $3.7B Medicare fraud scheme after capture in Turkey
events.description: Fugitive Ibrahim Khaldoon Hilmi captured in Turkey, after allegedly masterminding a $3.7 billion Medicare fraud scheme, one of the largest in U.S. history.

events.title: Florida man allegedly recorded girls in church bathroom with hidden camera, arrested on voyeurism charges
events.description: A Florida church overseer allegedly hid cameras in a bathroom and changing room, leading to video voyeurism charges after images were found on his laptop.

events.title: Former Gambino associate turned New Jersey councilman accused of running loansharking ring while in office
events.description: Former Gambino crime family associate turned New Jersey councilman John Alite allegedly ran an illegal loansharking ring while in office, authorities say.

events.title: Trump gets major win against China in African rare earth minerals race
events.description: Trump administration backs American mining company Virtus Minerals in the DRC to counter China's control of critical cobalt and copper supply chains.

events.title: Trump says Colombia's 'El Tigre' will be a 'great president' as socialist opponent launches legal challenge
events.description: Trump congratulated de la Espriella on his apparent Colombia election win. With 99.9% counted, he leads 49.7% to 48.7%, but rival Cepeda has launched a challenge over voting irregularities.

events.title: Keith Kellogg tells Iranian dissidents the 'window is open' to force regime change in Tehran
events.description: Lt. Gen. Keith Kellogg urged Iranian dissidents at a Paris NCRI gathering to seize a historic opening, saying the Tehran regime is weaker than ever.

events.title: Meloni's spat with Trump is calculated strategy to boost her approval ratings: expert
events.description: Analyst says Meloni's public feud with President Trump is a calculated strategy to boost her declining approval ratings ahead of Italy's 2027 general election.

events.title: Iran's unprecedented 'whole-regime' delegation at US deal talks signals one goal: expert
events.description: Iran sent a whole-regime delegation, including its central bank governor and oil officials to Switzerland talks, signaling demands for immediate cash flow.

events.title: Trump-backed 'El Tigre' looks to crush cartels, end Colombia's socialist era in pivotal election
events.description: Abelardo de la Espriella, known as El Tigre, rides voter frustration over crime and cartels in Colombia's high-stakes presidential runoff election.

events.title: African and Caribbean leaders call for payments, debt cancellation, formal apologies over slavery
events.description: African and Caribbean leaders adopted a 19-point reparations plan in Ghana demanding financial compensation, debt cancellation and formal apologies.

events.title: H5N1 bird flu confirmed in Australia for the first time, meaning virus has now reached every continent
events.description: Australia confirms its first case of H5N1 bird flu in a seabird near Esperance, Western Australia, meaning the virus has now reached every continent.

events.title: 'Send them back' chants rock European Parliament after landslide vote to speed up deportations
events.description: European Parliament approved the Return Regulation 418-218, sparking chants of "send them back" from right-wing lawmakers in a deeply divided vote.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ 'Send them back' chants rock European Parliament after landslide vote to speed up deportations
